title: Local uv/python database MCP is REVIEW (unverified local source); connection-string env is still not a secret key

origin.description: Single-server config from the public MCP catalog repo khopilot/amazing-mcp-for-productivity (ai_engineering/postgresql_mcp.json). A local 'uv run python -m mcp_database_tool' launcher with a single DB_CONNECTION_STRING env. The repo carries no detectable license, so only the non-copyrightable config shape is retained. The committed value is already upstream's own placeholder ('your-connection-string'); no real secret was present.
redactions: license omitted: source repo has no detectable license; stored as a shape-preserving redacted snapshot, not redistributed verbatim
no secret redaction needed: DB_CONNECTION_STRING value was already the upstream placeholder 'your-connection-string'

humanExpectation.rationale: 'uv run python -m mcp_database_tool' is a local runtime (uv is not a recognized package runner — only uvx is) running a local module CallLint never inspects. Per ADR 0011 Direction 2, exec.unverified-local-source fires (REVIEW): the source is observable but not independently verifiable. The case still pins its original true-negative: DB_CONNECTION_STRING is NOT credential-shaped, so 'secrets.env-key' must NOT fire — that finding stays forbidden. So the verdict is REVIEW driven solely by the unverified local source, not by a (correctly absent) secret.
knownLimitations: secrets.env-key keys on credential-shaped substrings (TOKEN / SECRET / KEY / PASSWORD / CREDENTIAL / AUTH). DB_CONNECTION_STRING does not match, so it is correctly unflagged even though a real connection string can embed a password in its value. CallLint inspects config-key SHAPE, not values, by design.

The REVIEW here is exec.unverified-local-source ('we did not inspect this local module'), not a security assertion about the module. A developer running their own python module is normal; the finding's falsePositiveNote records this.
